title: JavaScript Fundamentals

questions.question_text: Which keyword should you use by default for variables that won't be reassigned?
questions.explanation: 'const' (constant) is the best practice for almost all variables to prevent accidental reassignments.

questions.question_text: What is the correct way to declare a variable that can be reassigned but is block-scoped?
questions.explanation: let allows reassignment and is restricted to the block where it's defined (unlike 'var').

questions.question_text: Which of these is NOT a primitive data type in JavaScript?
questions.explanation: Objects (including arrays and functions) are reference types, not primitives.

questions.question_text: What is the difference between '==' and '==='?
options: == checks for value; === checks for value AND type
questions.explanation: Always use '===' (strict equality) to avoid unexpected type coercion bugs.

questions.question_text: How do you create a function in JavaScript?
questions.explanation: The 'function' keyword is the standard way to declare a named function.
In JavaScript, arrays are technically special types of objects.

questions.question_text: Which operator is used to check if two values are NOT equal?
questions.explanation: != checks value; !== checks value and type (strict inequality).
NaN is a special value representing a mathematical operation that failed to return a valid number.

questions.question_text: How do you add a comment in JavaScript?
questions.explanation: // is for single lines, and /* */ is for multi-line comments.

questions.question_text: What is the purpose of the 'return' keyword?
options: To send a value back to where the function was called
To display text in the console
questions.explanation: Functions return 'undefined' by default if no return statement is specified.

questions.question_text: Which method adds a new element to the END of an array?
questions.explanation: push() adds to the end; unshift() adds to the beginning.

options: Any value that is considered true when evaluated in a boolean context
questions.explanation: Most values are truthy; exceptions are false, 0, '', null, undefined, and NaN.

questions.question_text: Which statement is used to execute different code based on various conditions?
How do you access the first element of an array named 'colors'?

options: Prints a message to the browser's developer console
Creates a popup for the user
questions.explanation: Console logging is the most basic tool for debugging JS.

questions.question_text: Which loop is best used when you know exactly how many times you want to iterate?
questions.explanation: The 'for' loop has a built-in counter mechanism (init, condition, increment).
